beats.n_start: {HERO_FULL} steps off the last bus into {TOWN}, the {ORCHARD} town where she was born, and finds it half a stranger to itself; the {WAR_MEMORIAL} lists names no one in the {TOWN_SQUARE} can place, her cousin {COUSIN} waves at a house {HERO} remembers as a {BAKERY}, and when she says the word {BAKERY} three people give her the same polite blank look; only {HERO} seems to carry the missing years, the vanished shopfronts, the empty chairs still pulled up to kitchen tables, and four threads tug at her at once, the {REC_LOCATION} records, the last living {ORCHARD}-{KEEPER_ROLE} on {KEE_LOCATION}, the family {PHO_MEDIUM}s that will not stay the same, and the sealed {CELLAR} beneath the old {CIDER_HOUSE}
beats.rec_entry: The {REC_LOCATION} smells of dust and floor wax; the {REC_DOCUMENT} of {TOWN} births and deaths lies open, but whole pages are blank where names were inked, the paper faintly warm as if the words left only moments ago, and the young {REC_CHAR} keeps losing his place mid-sentence
beats.rec_a_p1: {HERO} leans across the counter and asks the {REC_CHAR} who lived at number nine on the {TOWN_SQUARE}, and watches him grope for a memory that keeps sliding out of reach
beats.rec_a_p2: The {REC_CHAR} whispers that he keeps a {PHO_MEDIUM} of a girl he is sure is his daughter, though he cannot summon her name or her face the moment he looks away from it
beats.rec_a_p3: {HERO} copies the blank entries into her own notebook, off the {REC_DOCUMENT}, hoping ink she carries out of {TOWN} will hold where the {REC_DOCUMENT} ink will not
beats.rec_a_p4: The {REC_CHAR} hands her a magnifier and together they find a birth entry where the name has thinned to a grey smear, present and absent at once
beats.rec_adec: {HERO} asks the {REC_CHAR} to say aloud a name he cannot hold, and his hands shake so hard the pen rolls off the desk, and she must choose between mercy and the answer she needs
beats.e_rec_a: The {REC_CHAR} panics, shoves {HERO} out and bolts the hall, and the one door into the {TOWN}s records closes against her
beats.rec_b_p1: The lease {REC_B_LEDGER} runs back two centuries in a dozen different hands, cracked and foxed, smelling of the same warm dust as the blank {REC_DOCUMENT}
beats.rec_b_p2: {HERO} traces the fortieth-year clause across the centuries and finds it always followed a hard season, a failed harvest, a fever, a flood, some grief the {TOWN} could not carry
beats.rec_b_p3: In the margin beside the newest clause someone has drawn a small tree with {ROOT}s far deeper than its branches, and inked beneath it the single word enough
beats.rec_b_p4: Every fortieth year the same clause reappears in careful script, a promise that the {ORCHARD} shall keep a little in exchange for a fruitful season
beats.rec_bdec: The repeating clause always names a {KEEPER_ROLE}, and {HERO} can read the line calmly forward or tear the whole page free in fright
beats.e_rec_b: Outside, the ink fades from the stolen page to nothing in {HERO} fist, her one hard fact turned to blank paper
beats.rec_conv: The last named {KEEPER_ROLE}, inked forty years ago, was {HERO}’s own {GRANDMOTHER}, and calmer now the {REC_CHAR} unlocks a back room where the forfeited years are shelved as sealed envelopes, one of them warm to the touch
beats.rec_t_p1: {HERO} breaks the warm seal and finds a {PHO_MEDIUM} of the {CIDER_HOUSE} {CELLAR} door standing open on darkness
beats.rec_t_p2: A second envelope holds a {REC_PHOTO} with a whole row of children scratched out, and a note reading they were the price of the year the mill burned
beats.rec_t_p3: {HERO} tucks the warm {PHO_MEDIUM}s inside her coat against her skin, as if her own heat could keep the images from fading like everything else in this {TOWN}
beats.rec_t_p4: Pencilled on the back of the first {PHO_MEDIUM} in a careful hand are the words it drinks from the {ROOT}, come before the next {DEADLINE}
beats.rec_clue: The envelope, the {REC_DOCUMENT}, the lease all point the same way, down to the {CIDER_HOUSE} {CELLAR} where something keeps the years
beats.kee_entry: {KEE_LOCATION} is all wind and bent apple trees, and at the top {KEE_CHAR_FULL}, the last {ORCHARD}-{KEEPER_ROLE}, sits pruning by feel because her eyes have gone; she knows {HERO} by the smell of the road on her, and says you are the one it cannot swallow, sit
beats.kee_a_p1: {KEE_CHAR} speaks of {PHENOMENON}, the slow forgetting that rises from the {OLDEST_TREE} when the {TOWN} forgets to feed it a name
beats.kee_a_p2: She tells how the {FOUNDERS} knelt at the {SPRING} in a starving winter and begged the {ORCHARD} to take their grief, and how it learned that grief is only memory with the edges still sharp
beats.kee_a_p3: Every {KEEPER_ROLE} since has fed it one willing name a year, and the last to do so before the line broke was {HERO}’s own {GRANDMOTHER}
beats.kee_a_p4: She asks whether {HERO} is brave or only stubborn, because the two look alike right up until the very end
beats.kee_adec: {KEE_CHAR} waits for {HERO} to say what she means to do about the hunger on the hill, whether to starve it blindly or learn its price first
beats.e_kee_a: {KEE_CHAR} decides {HERO} is a fool who will only make it hungrier, and sends her down the hill with a warning she cannot understand
beats.kee_b_p1: The {KEEPER_ROLE} keeps a knotted {KEEPER_CORD}, one knot for every {KEEPER_ROLE} back to the {FOUNDERS}, hung from a nail worn smooth by generations
beats.kee_b_p2: {KEE_CHAR} guides {HERO} thumb over each knot and names the {KEEPER_ROLE} it stands for, her voice steady until it reaches the knots no living mind can attach to a face
beats.kee_b_p3: Past those the {KEEPER_CORD} is smooth for a whole arm length, one lost {KEEPER_ROLE} after another, a rope of forgotten guardians holding a {TOWN} that does not know it is held
beats.kee_b_p4: The knots past a certain year are worn slick, as if handled by hands that no longer remember tying them
beats.kee_bdec: The {KEEPER_CORD} runs long in {HERO}’s hands, and she can trace the knots back to the founding with {KEE_CHAR}, or cut the line to try to end it
beats.e_kee_b: {HERO} saws the {KEEPER_ROLE} {KEEPER_CORD} in two and the loose knots scatter down the slope, cutting the last thread anyone held to the vanished years
beats.kee_conv: The {KEEPER_CORD} ends at her {GRANDMOTHER} knot, still tight and warm, with one fresh empty knot tied past it waiting for a name, and {KEE_CHAR} presses a withered {TOKEN_SEED} into {HERO} palm, the mark every {KEEPER_ROLE} carries
beats.kee_t_p1: {KEE_CHAR} says this is why {PHENOMENON} slides off you, you were never planted here, and describes the black {SPRING} under the {CIDER_HOUSE} where the founding {ROOT} learned to drink
beats.kee_t_p2: The {TOKEN_SEED} will green only in a hand {PHENOMENON} cannot touch, she says, and a green {TOKEN_SEED} is the {FOUNDERS} token, the sign that its bearer may bargain at the {SPRING}
beats.kee_t_p3: She cannot come down the hill on her own failing legs, but she promises to send {COUSIN} to the {CIDER_HOUSE} before the {DEADLINE} so {HERO} will not face the water alone
beats.kee_t_p4: She warns that the {ROOT} keeps what it takes and the first {DEADLINE} is only days away now
beats.kee_clue: {KEE_CHAR} last words send {HERO} to the {CELLAR}, where the oldest {ROOT} has cracked the founding wall and waits below in the dark
beats.pho_entry: In {COUSIN} {PHO_LOCATION} the family {PHO_MEDIUM}s will not hold still; a christening portrait loses a godmother between two blinks, a harvest crowd thins by a face each time {HERO} looks away, and the empty spaces heal over as if the missing were never there
beats.pho_a_p1: {HERO} lays every {PHO_MEDIUM} on the floorboards and pins the corners flat, determined to catch the erasure as it happens
beats.pho_a_p2: She films the pinned {PHO_MEDIUM}s on her phone, and on the small bright screen the vanishing happens slower, a face dimming frame by frame like a coal going grey
beats.pho_a_p3: When she plays the film back the erased faces are gone from the recording too, as if {PHENOMENON} reaches even into the memory of a machine that only borrowed the image
beats.pho_a_p4: For one heartbeat a woman stands at the {ORCHARD} gate, and then she is only a gap in the grass
beats.pho_adec: {HERO} can {PHO_MEDIUM} the {PHO_MEDIUM}s before they change, or panic and burn the fading ones to try to stop the bleeding
beats.e_pho_a: {HERO} sets a match to the fading prints, and the fire takes the last images of the forgotten, so not even the gaps remain to prove anyone was lost
beats.pho_b_p1: One {PHO_ALBUM} feels wrong in the hand, heavier, its images sharp and whole while everything around them fades
beats.pho_b_p2: The strange {PHO_ALBUM} has no people in it at all, only the {ORCHARD} through the seasons, the {OLDEST_TREE} standing a little taller and a little darker in every passing year
beats.pho_b_p3: Pressed between two pages {HERO} finds {PHO_KEEPSAKE} tied with thread, saved by someone who knew the pictures of people would not last
beats.pho_b_p4: Every picture in it is of the {ORCHARD}, the {CIDER_HOUSE}, the {OLDEST_TREE}, as if {PHENOMENON} guards its own face
beats.pho_bdec: {HERO} can compare the untouched {ORCHARD} {PHO_MEDIUM}s across the years, or close the strange {PHO_ALBUM} and walk away from it
beats.e_pho_b: {HERO} closes the strange {PHO_ALBUM}, telling herself {PHO_MEDIUM}s are only paper, and by morning cannot recall why the {PHO_LOCATION} mattered, the thread slipping even from her
beats.pho_conv: Laid in order the {ORCHARD} {PHO_MEDIUM}s show the {OLDEST_TREE} swelling year by year while the {TOWN} around it grows thin, and behind the last {PHO_MEDIUM}, tucked in the frame, is {HERO}’s {GRANDMOTHER}’s young face beside the {CELLAR} door holding the same withered {TOKEN_SEED}
beats.pho_t_p1: On the {PHO_ALBUM} final page, in her {GRANDMOTHER} hand, are the words go down to the {ROOT} before {DEADLINE}, it keeps the years there
beats.pho_t_p2: {HERO} compares the christening {PHO_MEDIUM} to the {PHO_ALBUM} and realises the missing godmother was a {KEEPER_ROLE}, remembered by the {ORCHARD} while the {TOWN} forgot her face
beats.pho_t_p3: She understands now why the {ORCHARD} {PHO_MEDIUM}s never change, the {ROOT} protects the image of the thing that feeds it, guarding its own long hunger against forgetting
beats.pho_t_p4: The {PHO_MEDIUM}s, whole and fading alike, all point {HERO} at the {CIDER_HOUSE} {CELLAR}
beats.pho_clue: The changing pictures and the whole ones agree, the answer waits below the {CIDER_HOUSE} where the {ROOT} drinks
beats.cel_entry: The {CIDER_HOUSE} leans empty at the {ORCHARD} edge, and its {CELLAR} door is sealed with a bar of {ORCHARD} wood grown over by living bark, as if the tree itself reached up to hold the door shut against anyone who would open it
beats.cel_a_p1: {HERO} works the {CEL_SEAL} bar loose splinter by splinter, and cold air breathes up from below
beats.cel_a_p2: The living bark bleeds a thin cider-smelling sap where her tools bite, and the whole door seems to flinch, wood shrinking from her like a hand pulled back from a flame
beats.cel_a_p3: Through the widening gap she can see the first steps slick with a {DEADLINE} that has no business being there in the mild autumn air of the {ORCHARD} above
beats.cel_a_p4: The draught carries a smell of apples and old water and something older underneath them both
beats.cel_adec: The open door gapes on darkness, and {HERO} can descend toward the pulsing light or lose her nerve and reseal it
beats.e_cel_a: {HERO} hammers the bark bar back into place and walks away, telling herself some doors are shut for good reason, and the {TOWN} keeps losing its years
beats.cel_b_p1: {HERO} drops a pebble down the dark stair and counts, and the count runs longer than the little building could ever hold
beats.cel_b_p2: The pebble never lands, or lands so far down that its knock returns as a whisper, and the cold that rises with the echo carries the smell of a hundred old kitchens
beats.cel_b_p3: She lowers her lamp on its cord and the flame leans sideways, drawn downward as if the dark itself were breathing in, patient and slow and endlessly hungry
beats.cel_b_p4: The steps descend past shelves of cider gone to vinegar toward a faint light that is not lamplight, pulsing slow like breath
beats.cel_bdec: {HERO} can follow the light down slowly, marking her steps, or retreat while she still remembers why she came
beats.e_cel_b: Halfway down {HERO} feels her own reason for coming thin and drain away, and she climbs back into daylight emptyhanded, the {CELLAR} having drunk her very purpose
beats.cel_conv: At the bottom the split wall lets the {OLDEST_TREE} taproot through, down into a black {SPRING}, and the water is not still, it holds faces and streets and seasons turning slowly in the deep
beats.cel_t_p1: The {SPRING} shows {HERO} her own reflection last of all, and hers alone does not dissolve into the drift of the drowned {TOWN}
beats.cel_t_p2: {HERO} kneels at the lip of the black water and the drowned faces turn up toward her lamp, mouths moving, asking to be remembered by anyone at all who still can
beats.cel_t_p3: Her {GRANDMOTHER}’s face drifts among them, younger than {HERO} ever knew her, and for one moment it steadies and seems to smile before the current turns it under again
beats.cel_t_p4: Scratched into the wet stone above the water are the words the {ROOT} keeps, the {DEADLINE} sets it, remember for us all
beats.cel_clue: The {CELLAR}, the {SPRING}, the drowned faces, every thread has led {HERO} to this same black water beneath the tree
beats.n_b1: However {HERO} came, every thread ends at the split founding wall beneath the {CIDER_HOUSE}, at the black {SPRING} where the {OLDEST_TREE} taproot drinks; here {PHENOMENON} has a shape at last, not a curse but a bargain, the {FOUNDERS} traded the {TOWN}s memory for a harvest that never fails, letting the {ROOT} keep a little each season, and the keeping has grown greedy, swallowing years and faces and whole lives while the {TOWN} smiles and forgets it ever had them; the {SPRING} turns with everything {TOWN} has lost, and {HERO} must decide how to face a thing that eats remembering itself
beats.b1a_p1: {HERO} cups the black water and it does not take her memory, it shows her more, the {FOUNDERS} kneeling at this {SPRING} long ago
beats.b1a_p2: The {SPRING} is warm as blood against her fingers and it does not pull at her, it only offers, showing the {FOUNDERS} faces smoothing as their grief is drawn away down into the {ROOT}
beats.b1a_p3: She sees a {KEEPER_ROLE} walk willingly into the {ROOT}s embrace so the {TOWN} could forget a grief too heavy to carry through a hard winter
beats.b1a_dec: The water offers {HERO} a deeper truth if she will hold it a moment longer, or she can draw her hand back cold and unchanged
beats.e_b1a: {HERO} holds the water too long and it shows her only the drowned faces turning, until she pulls back shaking, no wiser and much colder
beats.b1at_p1: The {SPRING} teaches her that the {ROOT} cannot hold what it never touched, and {HERO} grew up far from {TOWN}, her memories banked elsewhere
beats.b1at_p2: The lesson settles in her like cold water finding its level, that the girl the {TOWN} cannot swallow might be the one who can make it give back
beats.b1at_p3: That distance is why {PHENOMENON} slides off her, and it may be the one thing that lets her reach in and take memory back out
beats.b1b_p1: {HERO} tries to pack the split wall with stone and {ORCHARD} {B1B_CLAY}, but the {ROOT} only drinks around her hands, patient and unbothered
beats.b1b_p2: The {B1B_CLAY} she packs turns to slurry and runs between her fingers into the {SPRING}, and the {ROOT} drinks even that, taking the very stone she meant to stop it with
beats.b1b_p3: She understands you cannot wall off a hunger, you can only feed it or fill it with something it will keep
beats.b1b_dec: The greening {TOKEN_SEED} in {HERO} palm steadies the faces where she touches the water, and she can learn from that or keep uselessly mortaring the wall
beats.e_b1b: {HERO} mortars the wall shut and for one proud hour it holds, then the {ROOT} splits it wider than before and a whole childhood of the {TOWN} drains away in the night
beats.b1bt_p1: Where {HERO} untaken memory touches the {SPRING} the drowned faces steady, as if her outsideness were a stone the current cannot move
beats.b1bt_p2: She sees she has been trying to plug a mouth when she should have been meeting a need, and a need can be answered with a gift as easily as with a theft
beats.b1bt_p3: Stopping {PHENOMENON} is not about closing a door but about giving the {ROOT} something it can keep forever instead of the living
beats.b1c_p1: {HERO} runs up into the {TOWN_SQUARE} shouting the names from the blank {REC_DOCUMENT}, and a few townsfolk flinch as if the sounds fit old wounds
beats.b1c_p2: She grabs old {B1C_NEIGHBOR_NAME} by the sleeve and says your wife, {B1C_NEIGHBOR_SPOUSE}, the {B1C_NEIGHBOR_SHOP}, and for a heartbeat his eyes fill and then go smooth again, the grief wiped clean before it can land
beats.b1c_p3: Most only stare, unable to hold a grief for people they can no longer recall, their faces closing like shutters
beats.b1c_dec: {COUSIN} keeps his memory a moment longer than the rest, and {HERO} can build on his stubborn love or shout herself hoarse alone
beats.e_b1c: The {TOWN_SQUARE} empties around {HERO} as she shouts, and by dusk even {COUSIN} looks at her strangely, and she stands alone with a fist of names no one will take
beats.b1ct_p1: {COUSIN} tells her {PHENOMENON} is weakest where love is stubborn, where someone flatly refuses to let go of a face
beats.b1ct_p2: One held memory is a crack of light under a shut door, proof that {PHENOMENON} can be resisted if enough hands refuse to let go all at once
beats.b1ct_p3: If enough of the {TOWN} could be made to remember at once, on purpose, their held memory might anchor what the {ROOT} keeps stealing
beats.n_b2: The threads knot into one truth: {HERO} is immune because she grew up beyond {TOWN}, her memories rooted in soil {PHENOMENON} never owned, and that same outsideness is a key; she alone can reach into the {SPRING} and lift memory back out without being drunk down with it; {KEE_CHAR} withered {TOKEN_SEED}, greening now in her palm, is the {FOUNDERS} old token, the mark a {KEEPER_ROLE} carries, and it will let the {ROOT} recognise her as one who may bargain; but a bargain has a price, and the {ROOT} does not give years back for nothing, so {HERO} must learn what stopping {PHENOMENON} will cost before she can choose how much to pay
beats.b2t_p1: {HERO} offers the {SPRING} a small memory to test its hunger, the taste of her {GRANDMOTHER} kitchen on a Sunday
beats.b2t_p2: The returned {B2T_LANE} smells of coal smoke and Sunday baking, solid enough to walk down, and then the {ROOT} gently reminds her it is only lent, and the price is not yet paid
beats.b2t_p3: The {ROOT} takes it gently and gives back, for one breath, a whole vanished {B2T_LANE} of houses shimmering into being
beats.b2t_d1: So it is a trade, memory for memory, the beloved for the lost; {HERO} can measure the true cost or snatch her hand back now.
beats.e_b2ta: Frightened by how much the {ROOT} will take, {HERO} pulls free and keeps every memory clenched tight, and the {TOWN} goes on quietly losing itself around her
beats.b2t_m: The {ROOT} will hold whatever she gives it forever, meaning whatever she surrenders she can never have again; she can follow the cost fully or give something rashly.
beats.e_b2tb: {HERO} lets the {SPRING} take her {GRANDMOTHER} whole, and the {B2T_LANE} returns, but she stands in it a stranger, unable to recall why the houses should mean anything at all
beats.b2tt_p1: {HERO} understands the true price now, to restore the {TOWN} she must feed the {ROOT} something she loves enough to lose
beats.b2tt_p2: The arithmetic is plain and cruel, the {TOWN} can be bought back memory for memory, and the buyer must be someone {PHENOMENON} cannot bill for its own
beats.b2tt_p3: The more she is willing to give, the more of {TOWN} will come rising back out of the deep
beats.b2k_p1: {HERO} feels the fresh unnamed knot on {KEE_CHAR} {KEEPER_CORD} pull at her wrist, the empty {KEEPER_ROLE} place waiting to be filled
beats.b2k_p2: The empty knot warms against her wrist like a living thing, eager, and she feels how easy it would be to simply give herself and let the whole {TOWN} forget its grief in peace
beats.b2k_p3: She understands she could tie herself to it, become the one the {ORCHARD} remembers so the {TOWN} need not be devoured
beats.b2k_d1: A {KEEPER_ROLE} is remembered only by the {ROOT} and forgotten by everyone else; {HERO} can study that fate or refuse it outright.
beats.e_b2ka: {HERO} refuses the {KEEPER_ROLE} knot and walks away from the {KEEPER_CORD}, and without a {KEEPER_ROLE} the {ROOT} simply keeps eating, until by winter {KEE_CHAR} herself is a name {HERO} alone remembers
beats.b2k_m: The price is to be slowly erased from the living world even while holding it together from below; she can grasp the whole of it or tie the knot too soon in fear.
beats.e_b2kb: {HERO} ties the fresh knot around her own wrist before she understands the whole of it, and the {TOWN} steadies while she begins to fade from {COUSIN}’s eyes, a {KEEPER_ROLE} made in fear
beats.b2kt_p1: {HERO} sees the shape of the sacrifice, a {KEEPER_ROLE} anchors the {TOWN} by letting the {ORCHARD} keep her instead of the many
beats.b2kt_p2: A {KEEPER_ROLE} is loved only by the {ORCHARD} and lost to everyone above, and {HERO} is not ready to become a name that only a tree remembers, not yet, not in fear
beats.b2kt_p3: It is a life spent as the thing that remembers so that others may forget their grief in peace
beats.n_b3: Now {HERO} knows the price in full; the {ROOT} will give the {TOWN} back its stolen years, but only if it is given something to keep forever in their place, either a memory beloved enough to matter or a {KEEPER_ROLE} willing to be remembered by the {ORCHARD} and forgotten by the world; {KEE_CHAR} climbs down the {CELLAR} stair to stand with her, and {COUSIN} comes too, refusing to let {HERO} face the {SPRING} alone, and their being there changes the arithmetic, because a price paid by one is a wound and a price shared by many is only a scar; at the black {SPRING} beneath the {OLDEST_TREE}, with the {DEADLINE} gathering above, {HERO} must choose not whether to pay but how, and who pays alongside her
beats.b3u_p1: {HERO} sends {COUSIN} running through {TOWN} with the {REC_DOCUMENT} names, gathering everyone who will come to the {CIDER_HOUSE} before {DEADLINE}
beats.b3u_p2: {COUSIN} comes back breathless with a dozen townsfolk behind him, then a dozen more, drawn by a feeling they cannot name that tonight they must not forget on purpose
beats.b3u_p3: The plan is to make the {TOWN} remember together, out loud, all at once, so no single heart is drained to fill the {ROOT}
beats.b3u_d1: {HERO} can teach the {ROOT} a new bargain of shared keeping, or let the {TOWN} simply make peace with its smaller losses.
beats.e_b3un: {HERO} tells the gathered {TOWN} the {ORCHARD} was only lonely, and they choose to live with the losses, keeping what they have and mourning nothing they cannot name, a quiet peace with a quiet grief
beats.b3u_m: The weight must land on enough hands at once or it will crush the few who came; she can wait for all or rush the ritual.
beats.e_b3us: {HERO} begins before half the {TOWN} arrives, and the weight lands too heavy on too few, and the ones who came stagger back forgetting their own names, the chain snapping for want of hands
beats.b3ut_p1: {KEE_CHAR} shows {HERO} how to teach the {ROOT} a keeping spread across a hundred hearts rather than devouring one
beats.b3ut_p2: {KEE_CHAR}’s voice reaches them from the top of the stair, teaching the old words that turn a devouring into a keeping, a hunger fed by one into a memory held by all
beats.b3ut_p3: The living will hold the {TOWN} memory between them like a chain, and the {ROOT} will keep through many instead of hollowing anyone
beats.b3v_p1: {HERO} cannot empty the whole {SPRING}, so she chooses one, kneeling with a single face fixed in her mind
beats.b3v_p2: She fixes the {NEIGHBOR_GIRL} in her mind, the gap-toothed grin, the way she always won at {B3V_GAME}, a whole small life the {TOWN} lost the same winter {HERO} was taken away
beats.b3v_p3: It is that girl she means to lift back into the light no matter the cost
beats.b3v_d1: {HERO} can brace to trade a piece of her own heart for the one soul, or simply learn who she would have saved.
beats.e_b3vn: {HERO} asks the {SPRING} only to show her who she would save, and it lifts a face to the surface, and she carries the knowing back up unspent, changed by it, having taken nothing and given nothing
beats.b3v_m: The {ROOT} offers one memory of {HERO}’s own for one soul restored; she can pay the fair price or try to cheat it.
beats.e_b3vs: {HERO} tries to pull one soul free while surrendering nothing, and the {ROOT} closes hard, keeping both the lost one and the hour she wasted, and she surfaces having saved no one
beats.b3vt_p1: {HERO} braces to give up one precious thing so that one lost person may walk {TOWN} again, remembered by all
beats.b3vt_p2: One rescue, complete and clean, is a candle lit against a dark she cannot flood with light, and she decides a single saved life is not too small a thing to matter
beats.b3vt_p3: It is a small and complete mercy in a sea she knows she cannot empty by herself
beats.b3w_p1: {HERO} decides she does not need to defeat {PHENOMENON}, only to understand it well enough to live honestly beside it
beats.b3w_p2: The {ROOT} shows her the {FOUNDERS} not as villains but as parents who could not bury one more child and still rise to prune the trees, and who made a terrible mercy of forgetting
beats.b3w_p3: She asks the {ROOT} not for years but for the whole truth of the bargain the {FOUNDERS} made in their grief
beats.b3w_d1: {HERO} can accept an even trade for a single year, or try to force the whole hard truth into the {TOWN} at once.
beats.e_b3wn: {HERO} accepts the old bargain and asks for a single year back, the year she was born here, and the {ROOT} gives it and keeps a year of hers, a fair and bittersweet swap that leaves her both fuller and lighter
beats.b3w_m: The {ROOT}s long memory is a grief no one mind can hold alone; she can carry it wisely or pour it into the {TOWN} uninvited.
beats.e_b3ws: {HERO} tries to pour the {ROOT}s long memory into every mind in {TOWN}, and the {TOWN} reels, unable to bear a grief handed to them whole, and they turn from her, choosing forgetting over a truth too heavy
beats.b3wt_p1: The {SPRING} opens its long memory to {HERO}, the hard winters, the mercy and the theft tangled together as one old unhealed wound
beats.b3wt_p2: She means to meet the {OLDEST_TREE} the way you meet an old grief in the family, not to defeat it but to finally say aloud the names it has carried alone for two hundred years
beats.b3wt_p3: Carrying that understanding, she means to face the {OLDEST_TREE} not as an enemy but as the {KEEPER_ROLE} of a sorrow the whole {TOWN} must finally own
beats.n_b4: Every path brings {HERO} to the same place at last, the black {SPRING} beneath the {OLDEST_TREE} with the first {DEADLINE} silvering the {ORCHARD} overhead, the {TOWN} gathered on the stair or a single face held in her mind; the withered {TOKEN_SEED} in her palm is green and whole now, the {KEEPER_ROLE} token alive again, and the {ROOT} waits, ancient and patient, ready to make its bargain; this is the hinge of everything, the moment {PHENOMENON} can be turned from a hunger into a keeping, from a theft into a shared remembering, or lost for another forty years; whatever {HERO} carries down to the water now, the {TOWN} memory, a single name, an honest grief, she must offer it here and see what the {ORCHARD} chooses to give back
beats.fin_all_1: {HERO} presses the greening {TOKEN_SEED} to the black water and speaks the {FOUNDERS} bargain backward, not the {ORCHARD} keeps for us but we keep for the {ORCHARD}, and the {ROOT} shudders as a hundred living memories pour down the {CELLAR} stair at once
beats.e_all_flood: The flood of remembering is more than {HERO} can steer alone, and it scatters before it can anchor, leaving the {TOWN} dazed and the {ROOT} fed just enough to sleep, the great chance spent for a fragile partial calm
beats.fin_all_2: With {KEE_CHAR}’s hands over hers and {COUSIN}’s voice leading the crowd, {HERO} teaches the {ROOT} the new bargain, and the {SPRING} stops turning like a drain and begins to turn like a wheel, holding the {TOWN}’s memory for it rather than swallowing it
beats.fin_share_1: {HERO} asks the gathered {TOWN} not to give up anything they love but to promise together, aloud, to remember, and the vow rises off them like warmth off a fire, and the {ROOT} leans toward it, curious at a thing offered freely
beats.e_share_lost: The crowds nerve fails as the {SPRING} stirs, and one by one they climb back toward the light, and {HERO} is left holding a vow no one will finish speaking, the {ROOT} settling back to its slow patient theft
beats.fin_share_2: The shared vow spreads thin across every heart in the {CELLAR} so no single person is drained, and the {ROOT}, offered a keeping it can hold without hollowing anyone, accepts the new bargain the way dry ground accepts rain
beats.n_b5: The {ROOT} accepts the shared keeping and the split wall knits shut with living bark, and up in the {ORCHARD} the vanished years come rising back into the {TOWN} like dawn filling a valley, streets, faces, the {BAKERY}, the {NEIGHBOR_GIRL}, all of it remembered at once by everyone who chose to hold it
beats.e_all_restore: {TOWN} wakes whole, every stolen year restored and now held safe by a {TOWN} that remembers on purpose, the {ORCHARD} bearing fruit without eating its {KEEPER_ROLE}s, and {HERO} stands in a {TOWN_SQUARE} full of names that finally fit their faces, the girl who taught a {TOWN} to keep itself
beats.n_b5_2: One face stays missing, {HERO}’s own {GRANDMOTHER}, the last {KEEPER_ROLE}, whose memory the {ROOT} has held longest and deepest, and {HERO} realises she can leave her in peace or reach once more for the one soul the flood passed by
beats.e_all_grandmother: {HERO} reaches one last time and lifts her {GRANDMOTHER} up out of the deep water, and the old {KEEPER_ROLE} opens her eyes in a {TOWN} that suddenly remembers loving her, and the two of them stand in the restored {ORCHARD}, the long debt paid and the family whole again
beats.e_all_rest: {HERO} lets her {GRANDMOTHER} rest where she chose to be, remembered now by the {ROOT} as the {KEEPER_ROLE} who gave the {TOWN} its second chance, and {TOWN} lives on whole around that one gentle absence, a scar it will honour instead of forget
beats.fin_one_1: {HERO} holds one face steady in her mind, the {NEIGHBOR_GIRL} lost the winter she left, and offers the {ROOT} a single memory of her own in fair trade for that one life lifted back into the {TOWN}
beats.e_one_hesitate: {HERO} hesitates too long over which piece of herself to give, and the {DEADLINE} closes over the {SPRING} for another year, and the {NEIGHBOR_GIRL} stays a face in a fading {PHO_MEDIUM}, so close to saved that the near miss aches
beats.fin_one_2: {HERO} gives up her own first summer in {TOWN}, a small bright thing she can spare, and the {ROOT} takes it gently and lifts the {NEIGHBOR_GIRL} up out of the water, blinking, alive, remembered again by a {TOWN} that mourned her without knowing why
beats.e_one_saved: {HERO} climbs the {CELLAR} stair with the {NEIGHBOR_GIRL} beside her into a {TOWN} that gasps to remember her, and though {PHENOMENON} still sleeps below and the wider loss remains, one whole life walks {TOWN} again, bought back with a summer {HERO} will never miss as much as this is worth
beats.e_one_content: The {ROOT} will give no more than the fair one for one, and {HERO} accepts it, choosing not to bleed herself dry chasing everyone, and she walks home with the {NEIGHBOR_GIRL} and a hard gentle wisdom, that saving one completely is worth more than failing at saving all
beats.fin_trade_1: {HERO} kneels at the water not to win but to be honest with it, and she offers the {ROOT} her own memory of the day she first understood the {TOWN} was disappearing, trading her certainty for a single year given back to {TOWN}
beats.e_trade_greed: {HERO} asks for more years than she offered, and the {ROOT}, patient and exact, keeps what she gave and grants nothing, and she surfaces poorer by one memory and richer by no restored year, taught the cost of grasping
beats.fin_trade_2: The {ROOT} takes {HERO} certainty and gives back the year she asked for, the {BAKERY} on the {TOWN_SQUARE} with its warm lit window, and she understands she has not defeated {PHENOMENON} but made an honest peace with it, a fair exchange between a girl and an old grief
beats.e_trade_even: {HERO} climbs into a {TOWN} with one more street than it had that morning, carrying a little less of herself and a little more of {TOWN}, and settles into the bittersweet knowledge that some things return only when we agree to let other things go
beats.e_trade_keeper_light: {HERO} promises the {ROOT} a yearly visit, a {KEEPER_ROLE} who tends rather than surrenders, trading a single memory each {DEADLINE} for a single year returned, and she chooses to spend her life this way, slowly buying {TOWN} back one season at a time, neither wholly winning nor losing
beats.e_trade_photo: Unwilling to feed the {ROOT} anything she truly loves, {HERO} keeps back one thing it can never take, a single {PHO_MEDIUM} of the {ORCHARD} gate held so tight it creases, and she walks home with the {TOWN} still thinning around her, one saved image of everything lost

n_start.c_n_start_1: Search the {REC_LOCATION} for the missing years.
n_start.c_n_start_3: Lay out the family {PHO_MEDIUM}s that keep changing.

rec_bdec.c_rec_bdec_1: Trace the clause forward in the {REC_B_LEDGER}.

rec_clue.c_rec_clue_1: Carry what you found to the {CIDER_HOUSE}.

kee_bdec.c_kee_bdec_1: Let {KEE_CHAR} guide your fingers along the knots.
kee_bdec.c_kee_bdec_2: Cut the {KEEPER_CORD} to break the line.

kee_clue.c_kee_clue_1: Carry what you found to the {CIDER_HOUSE}.

pho_entry.c_pho_entry_2: Search for a {PHO_MEDIUM} that will not change.

pho_clue.c_pho_clue_1: Carry what you found to the {CIDER_HOUSE}.

cel_clue.c_cel_clue_1: Carry what you found to the {CIDER_HOUSE}.

n_b1.c_n_b1_1: Reach into the {SPRING} to understand it.
n_b1.c_n_b1_2: Refuse it and try to wall the {ROOT} off.
n_b1.c_n_b1_3: Run and call {TOWN} to witness this.

n_b2.c_n_b2_1: Weigh giving the {ROOT} a memory of your own.
n_b2.c_n_b2_2: Weigh taking the empty {KEEPER_ROLE}s place yourself.

b2k_m.c_b2k_m_2: Tie yourself to the {KEEPER_ROLE} knot right now.

n_b3.c_n_b3_1: Ask the whole {TOWN} to share the weight.
n_b3.c_n_b3_2: Offer the {ROOT} one thing to save one soul.

b3u_d1.c_b3u_d1_2: Let {TOWN} forgive the {ORCHARD} and go home.

b3v_d1.c_b3v_d1_2: Only ask the {ROOT} to show you her face.

b3v_m.c_b3v_m_2: Demand the {ROOT} give without taking anything.

b3w_d1.c_b3w_d1_2: Take back one year on the {ROOT}s own terms.

n_b4.c_n_b4_1: Anchor the whole {TOWN}s memory at once.
n_b4.c_n_b4_2: Bind the shared vow of the living into the {ROOT}.

fin_all_2.c_fin_all_2_1: Seal the new keeping into the {ROOT}.

n_b5.c_n_b5_1: Watch {TOWN} wake into its whole self.

fin_trade_1.c_fin_trade_1_2: Grasp for more than the {ROOT} will give.
